rationale: Dinner et al. (2014) is used beyond a passing background mention: it appears in the literature gap setup, in hypothesis positioning, and again in interpretation of results. The authors explicitly state they extend prior findings from Dinner et al. by ruling out a lagged-information explanation and documenting a brand-building cross-campaign effect. They also draw on Dinner et al. in model construction (ad-stock carryover parameterization), indicating meaningful conceptual and empirical dependence.
relation_description: The citing paper explicitly builds on Dinner et al. (2014)'s cross-channel advertising findings by adding consumer-segment heterogeneity (recent vs nonrecent online consumers) and separating within-campaign versus cross-campaign effects. It also examines website-visit mediation and compares its long-term banner effects against Dinner et al.'s estimates.
